title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sugar cane's Pacific origin
description: Papua New Guinea — Saccharum officinarum domesticated ~10,000 years ago. Spreads across Oceania via Austronesian migrations.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sugar cane's Pacific origin
description: India ~500 BCE — first documented crystallized sugar production ('khanda' gives us 'candy').

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sugar cane's Pacific origin
description: Persia + Levant — Arab agricultural revolution spreads cane cultivation ~700 CE.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sugar cane's Pacific origin
description: Andalusia (Spain) — Moorish agricultural knowledge introduces cane to Iberia 8th–10th century.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 1)
description: Portugal/Spain — European start. Manufactured goods: textiles, guns, metal goods board ships bound for West Africa.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 1)
description: West African coast (Elmina, Bight of Benin) — ~12.5 million enslaved Africans forced onto ships 1501–1866 (slavevoyages.org).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 1)
description: Saint-Domingue (Haiti) — 'the richest sugar colony on Earth' by 1788, built on ~500,000 enslaved people.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 1)
description: Barbados — first major British sugar colony (1640s).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 1)
description: Jamaica — second-largest British sugar producer.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 1)
description: Bahia Brazil — Portuguese sugar engenho (mill) economy, ~40% of all enslaved Africans brought to the Americas.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 1)
description: New Orleans (Louisiana) — US sugar plantation economy; 'sold down the river' originates here.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 2)
description: Boston + New England — molasses imports → rum distilleries → ships back to West Africa. The triangular trade closes.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Atlantic Slave Trade Triangle (leg 3)
description: London — refined sugar and rum return to Europe. Bristol + Liverpool + London grow wealthy on the triangle.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sugar beet revolution (1747+)
description: Berlin — Andreas Marggraf identifies sucrose in sugar beets 1747. First factory (Silesia) 1802.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sugar beet revolution (1747+)
description: Paris — Napoleonic Continental System blockade (1806) drives French investment in beet sugar. Ends Europe's dependency on Caribbean cane.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Sugar beet revolution (1747+)
description: Russia — beet sugar industry dominates Eastern European supply by mid-19th century.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Sanskrit 'sharkara' → sugar
description: India — Sanskrit 'sharkara' (शर्करा) meaning 'grit' or 'gravel' — describes the crystallized form.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Sanskrit 'sharkara' → sugar
description: Arabic — 'sukkar' (سكر). Medieval Islamic traders spread the commodity + the word.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Sanskrit 'sharkara' → sugar
description: Italian — 'zucchero'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Sanskrit 'sharkara' → sugar
description: Spanish — 'azúcar'. Preserves the Arabic 'al-' article.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Sanskrit 'sharkara' → sugar
description: French — 'sucre'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: Sanskrit 'sharkara' → sugar
description: English — 'sugar'. Separately — 'candy' comes from 'khanda' (a piece).

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: São Paulo Brazil — 39% of world cane sugar + world's largest ethanol-from-cane producer.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Uttar Pradesh India — 20% of world production.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Guangxi + Yunnan China — 6% of world production.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Thailand — 5% of world production, world's #2 exporter.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Queensland Australia — major exporter to East Asian markets.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: USA corn belt — high-fructose corn syrup replaces cane sugar in most processed food post-1980 due to US tariff policy.
